title: Login Dialog at Thunderbird startup for "https://caldav.gmx.net"
creator: willma.waswissen
content:
Dear all,

For aprox. 3 weeks now Thunderbird asks me a short time after startup for a login.

It shows up a dialog with the text : "https://caldav.gmx.net verlangt einen Benutzernamen und ein Passwort."

The problem is, that I maintain several GMX - accounts and I've no idea, which credentials I have to enter :/

How can I find out ( without inspecting or debugging the source code ;-) )

THX in advance and best regards!

title: Da circa una settimana Thunderbird è diventato lento e spesso si blocca per parecchi secondi
creator: dottormessina
content: Buongiorno a tutti, da qualche giorno la gestione di Thunderbird è diventata difficile, per aprire messaggi o impostazioni si blocca visualizzando la clessidra per parecchi secondi. Ho la versione 151.0.1 (64 bit), come estensione ho aggiunto Textmarker (ma da parecchi mesi). Adesso ho provato a svuotare la cashe, ho eliminato il file global.sqlite e riavviato, ho deframmentato il PC, e lascerò attivo il programma perchè ricostruisca tutti i suoi indici. Avete altri suggerimenti? Grazie

title: How to change view column widths to fixed size
creator: fred.d1
content:
I use vertical view. Hate threading so that's turned off. I use a medium to large message pane. Recently been having issues with column visibility. Not sure if this is new or whether it's that I've come to a Linux client from a Windows one and there's a setting I missed when I set up the client.

Essentially dragging the column divider doesn't work properly (for me). The columns always seem to auto adjust width. Similarly if I resize the message pane the columns don't remain fixed they auto adjust.

I don't find this helpful. I prefer to set up the columns to display the amount I want/need in subject and From and I truncate the date to simply not display time.

How do I set column widths to remain where I put them? Or is this function now lost.

title: How to fix threading
creator: lordthistle
content:
Thunderbird aggregates in the same threads e-mails from my employer that are completely unrelated. They are sent in different months, with a different subject, etc. and they end up in the same thread. Clearly, there is something in the header pushing Thunderbird to aggregate them, but this results in a very annoying ordering of the emails.

In my case, e-mail messages are sorted on date with descending order. However, the first message I see in my list dates back to months ago because another message sent few days ago has been merged with the previous one in a thread.

This would not be a problem if the message displayed in the list were the last one added to the thread and not the first one starting the thread.

I have done several searches and read several help pages and discussions here, but I cannot find a way to display threads in the same way the rest of the messages is shown, i.e. latest first. For some obscure reasons, Thunderbird keeps displaying in the message list and in the threaded view (when I expand the thread), the messages in reverse order wrt my selection.

How can I fix this?
